questions.question: What is a function prototype in C++?
choices: The complete function with its body
A declaration that specifies the function's return type, name, and parameter types before its definition
A comment describing what the function does
A function that has no return value
questions.explanation: A function prototype declares the function's return type, name, and parameter types so the compiler knows about the function before it is defined. It ends with a semicolon.

questions.question: What happens to the value of a local variable when the function it is defined in finishes executing?
choices: It is saved for the next function call
It is destroyed and its memory is released
It is returned to the calling function automatically
questions.explanation: Local variables exist only during the execution of the function in which they are defined. When the function ends, local variables are destroyed.

questions.question: What is function overloading in C++?
choices: Calling a function too many times
Having two or more functions with the same name but different parameter lists
Passing too many arguments to a function
questions.explanation: Function overloading allows multiple functions to share the same name as long as they have different parameter lists (different number or types of parameters).

questions.question: In pass by value, what happens when a variable is passed as an argument to a function?
choices: The function receives the memory address of the variable
A copy of the variable's value is passed to the function's parameter
The original variable is modified directly
The variable is deleted after the function call
questions.explanation: Pass by value means a copy of the argument's value is made and assigned to the function's parameter. Changes to the parameter inside the function do not affect the original argument.

questions.question: A function that does not return a value has a return type of ___.
questions.explanation: The void keyword indicates that a function does not return a value. Void functions perform actions but do not send a value back to the calling code.

questions.question: A variable defined outside all functions is called a ___ variable.
questions.explanation: Global variables are defined outside of all functions and are accessible from any function in the program. Their use is generally discouraged because they make programs harder to debug.
